description: Improve audio-visual synchronization with crossfade transitions, audio normalization, background music with auto-ducking, and subtitle/caption generation synced to audio timestamps
steps: Step 1: Add audio-related models in backend/app/models/schemas.py - Create AudioSettings model with fields: crossfade_duration (float, default 0.5s), normalize_audio (bool, default True), background_music_url (optional string), background_music_volume (float 0-1, default 0.15), enable_ducking (bool, default True for auto-ducking when narration plays), generate_subtitles (bool, default False). Add AudioSettings as optional field to AssembleVideoRequest and GenerateFullRequest.

Step 2: Create backend/app/services/audio_processor.py - Implement audio processing functions: (a) normalize_audio_clip(clip) - uses ffmpeg-normalize or manual gain adjustment to normalize volume across all scene audio clips to a consistent dBFS level. (b) apply_crossfade(clips, duration) - applies audio crossfade between consecutive scene clips. (c) mix_background_music(scene_clips, music_path, volume, enable_ducking) - downloads background music, loops it to match total duration, and mixes it with narration; when ducking is enabled, reduce music volume during narration segments. (d) generate_subtitle_data(tts_results, script) - creates subtitle timing data (SRT format) from TTS durations and narration text.

Step 3: Update backend/app/services/video_assembler.py - Integrate the audio_processor: Before concatenating scenes, normalize audio if enabled. Use crossfade_duration from AudioSettings in the concatenate_videoclips padding parameter (replacing hardcoded -0.5). After assembly, mix background music if URL provided. Generate .srt subtitle file alongside the video if generate_subtitles is True. Add subtitle_path to VideoResult.
Step 4: Update backend/app/models/schemas.py VideoResult - Add optional subtitle_path field.
Step 5: Add new endpoint in backend/app/routers/videos.py - Add GET /api/videos/{video_id}/subtitles to serve the generated .srt file.
Step 6: Update frontend/src/types/index.ts - Add AudioSettings interface and add audio_settings to VideoRequest. Add subtitle_path to VideoResult.

Step 7: Update frontend/src/components/QualitySettings.tsx (or create AudioSettingsPanel.tsx) - Add audio controls section with toggles for normalize audio, subtitle generation, crossfade duration slider, and an optional background music URL input.
Step 8: Update backend/tests/test_schemas.py - Add tests for AudioSettings model validation.
acceptance_criteria: AudioSettings model validates all fields with correct defaults and constraints
Audio normalization function adjusts volume levels across scenes
Crossfade duration is configurable and applied between scenes
Background music mixing with auto-ducking works when a music URL is provided
Subtitle .srt file is generated with accurate per-scene timing when enabled
Subtitles endpoint serves the .srt file for a given video_id
All existing backend tests continue to pass
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/abetVideos/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v' and confirm all tests pass
Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/abetVideos/frontend && npm run build' and confirm no TypeScript compilation errors
findings: All 68 backend tests pass (18 new tests for AudioSettings, VideoResult with subtitle_path, and AssembleVideoRequest/GenerateFullRequest with audio_settings). Frontend TypeScript builds cleanly. AudioSettings model has proper field constraints (crossfade_duration ge=0.0 le=2.0, background_music_volume ge=0.0 le=1.0). The audio_settings field is optional with None default on both AssembleVideoRequest and GenerateFullRequest, maintaining full backward compatibility. The subtitles endpoint serves .srt files at GET /api/videos/{video_id}/subtitles.
